methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $110M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Manna Aero is a Series B-stage drone and autonomous systems company that has raised $110M to date. The relevant peer universe consists of seven private companies in the Drones & Autonomous sector, including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Asylon Robotics. No public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

Given the sparsity of same-stage and adjacent-stage peers with sufficient data granularity, the Peer-Median method was not applicable. Instead, Stage-Progression analysis was employed, applying a 1.9x multiplier to the $110M total raised proxy to estimate post-Series B valuation. This methodology reflects typical step-up dynamics from Series B to later funding rounds and yielded an implied valuation of $209M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $178M to $240M, with a median point estimate of $209M. This range incorporates downside and upside sensitivities around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient comparable transaction data within the narrow Drones & Autonomous sector at equivalent stage and scale, limiting the ability to cross-validate via peer multiples. Additionally, valuation sensitivity to near-term milestones in autonomous vehicle certification and deployment metrics remains material but is not fully captured in the current dataset.
